title: OpenClaw resolveGroupToolPolicy accepted unvalidated caller-supplied group IDs
description: In src/agents/pi-tools.policy.ts the resolveGroupToolPolicy function built its candidate group-id list by directly trusting the caller-supplied params.groupId (via buildScopedGroupIdCandidates(params.groupId)) merged with server-derived session/spawn context group ids, then resolved and returned group-scoped tool policy for those ids. The trust check that validates a caller-provided groupId against the session-derived group context (resolveTrustedGroupId) lived only in one caller, applyFinalEffectiveToolPolicy in pi-embedded-runner/effective-tool-policy.ts, so any other caller of resolveGroupToolPolicy could supply an arbitrary group id and have group-scoped policy resolved for it, applying the wrong group-policy decision for a tool invocation.
